questions.question:
Grammar 1: Choose the correct option.

If she _____ harder last semester, she would have passed the exam.

Grammar 2: Choose the correct option.

Neither the teacher nor the students _____ ready for the surprise quiz.

Grammar 3: Choose the correct option.

The report, along with the appendixes, _____ submitted yesterday.

Grammar 4: Choose the correct option.

I wish I _____ more time to revise before the interview.

Grammar 5: Choose the correct option.

She asked me where _____ the previous evening.

Grammar 6: Choose the correct option.

By the time we arrive, the lecture _____.

Grammar 7: Choose the correct option.

It is essential that every student _____ the form carefully.

Grammar 8: Choose the correct option.

The more you practice speaking, _____ confident you become.

Grammar 9: Choose the correct option.

He is used to _____ early for morning classes.

Grammar 10: Choose the correct option.

Not only _____ late, but he also forgot his assignment.

Vocabulary 1: Choose the word closest in meaning to the underlined word.

The professor's explanation was very __lucid__, so everyone understood the topic.

Vocabulary 2: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.

Many students find it difficult to _____ between similar-sounding words.

Vocabulary 3: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.

The university decided to _____ the old language lab with modern equipment.

Vocabulary 4: Choose the word closest in meaning to the underlined word.

Her argument was __compelling__ and persuaded most of the audience.

Vocabulary 5: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.

Good time management is _____ for success in intensive language courses.

Vocabulary 6: Choose the word that is the opposite of the underlined word.

The instructions were __ambiguous__, so students asked many questions.

Vocabulary 7: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.

After reviewing the draft, the tutor asked the student to _____ the conclusion.

Vocabulary 8: Choose the best collocation.

She made a _____ effort to improve her writing over the summer.

Reading Passage

Many universities now require students to take an English proficiency exam before admission. These tests usually measure reading, writing, listening, and speaking. However, recent research suggests that daily practice may improve scores more effectively than occasional long study sessions.

According to language experts, short and regular study habits help learners retain vocabulary and grammar patterns. For example, practicing for 45–60 minutes every day can be more useful than studying for five hours only once a week. Consistency also reduces test anxiety because students become familiar with question types.

Technology has made daily practice easier. Online platforms provide timed exercises, instant feedback, and progress tracking. Still, experts warn that technology alone is not enough. Students should also review mistakes carefully and ask teachers for guidance on weak areas. Without reflection, repeated practice may simply reinforce errors.

Parents and teachers can support learners by helping them set realistic goals. A clear weekly plan—such as two grammar days, two reading days, one writing day, and one review day—can make preparation more manageable. Ultimately, success depends less on last-minute effort and more on steady improvement over time.

---
Reading 1: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: English exams are unnecessary for university admission.
Daily practice is more effective than irregular long study sessions.
questions.correct: Daily practice is more effective than irregular long study sessions.

questions.question: Reading 2 (same passage): According to the passage, why can daily practice reduce test anxiety?
options: Students become familiar with question types.
Parents take the test for them.
questions.correct: Students become familiar with question types.

questions.question: Reading 3 (same passage): What do experts say about technology?
options: It is useless for language learning.
It is helpful but not enough by itself.
It should replace all classroom teaching.
It only helps with listening skills.
questions.correct: It is helpful but not enough by itself.

questions.question: Reading 4 (same passage): What may happen if students practice without reviewing mistakes?
options: They will finish the exam early.

questions.question: Reading 5 (same passage): Which weekly plan is mentioned as an example?
options: Two grammar days, two reading days, one writing day, and one review day
One long session on the weekend only
Listening practice five times a day
questions.correct: Two grammar days, two reading days, one writing day, and one review day

questions.question: Reading 6 (same passage): The word "retain" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:

Writing Task (about 20–25 minutes)

Some people believe that students should practice English every day for short periods. Others think long study sessions a few times a week are better.

Write an essay of about 150–200 words.
- Give your opinion
- Support it with reasons and examples
- Write in clear paragraphs

Tip: Check grammar, vocabulary, and organization before you finish.
